zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：弱势震荡下的均线压制结构
zh.content:
当前SOL/USD整体处于均线压制下的弱势整理结构，价格运行在66.22附近，明显低于20日均线68.59，短期反弹动能不足，市场偏向震荡偏空格局，需观察是否形成底部企稳信号。

技术指标方面，MACD维持负值-1.00附近运行，动能柱未见明显收敛，RSI数据缺失但从价格窄幅波动看，市场情绪偏谨慎，多空力量暂未出现有效反转信号。

支撑位关注65.90一线短期低点，其下方可延伸至65.50区域；上方阻力首先在68.60均线位置，若突破则进一步看向70.00整数关口压力。

操作上建议以反弹做空思路为主，未突破68.60前保持谨慎偏空策略，若跌破65.90需防加速下探，止损可设于区间上沿以控制风险。

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
SOL/USD is currently trading around 66.22, showing a mildly bearish short-term structure as price remains below the 20-day SMA at 68.59. The market has been consolidating after recent downside pressure, with limited volatility reflected in the narrow 24-hour range. Overall trend bias leans downward to neutral, as buyers have not yet reclaimed key moving average territory or generated sustained breakout momentum above local highs, with volatility compressing across intraday sessions.

MACD remains in negative territory at -1.00, indicating persistent bearish momentum despite short-term stabilization in price action. With the signal line unavailable, confirmation is limited, but histogram behavior suggests weakening downside pressure rather than full reversal. RSI data is not provided, yet price behavior implies mid-to-low neutral momentum. The lack of strong bullish divergence keeps upside attempts constrained near resistance.

Immediate support is seen near the recent low at 65.90, with a deeper cushion around 65.00 if selling pressure resumes. On the upside, the first resistance aligns with the 20-day SMA at 68.59, followed by a stronger barrier near 70.00 where prior rejection occurred. Price action suggests a compression phase, where breakout direction will likely define the next short-term trend.

Given the current structure, a cautious approach is favored. Traders may look for long entries only on confirmed reclaim of 68.59 with volume support, targeting 70.00 and above. Alternatively, short setups remain viable on rejection near resistance zones, especially if price fails to hold above 67.00 intraday. Risk management is critical, with tight stops below 65.90 to avoid breakdown exposure.
